_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Ability to apply tradecraft to minimize risk of detection, mitigate risk, and minimize creation of behavioral signature
reference_document_element_description: Skill in designing risk appetite statements for data and AI activities that are specific enough to guide operational risk decisions yet flexible enough to accommodate business evolution and emerging use cases.
comments: DCWF ksa intersects with WIDAI RM domain. RM-S-008 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: RC-A-001 (RC, cosine: 0.367), RC-A-005 (RC, cosine: 0.348).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark.
primary_method.significance: STS 5.5/10 (raw 0.555). Shared vocabulary: [risk]. DCWF-only terms [ability, apply, behavioral, creation, detection, minimize] vs WIDAI-only terms [accommodate, activities, ai, appetite, business, cases] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.854 (P=0.863, R=0.844). Balanced P/R — the 17-word DCWF element and 31-word WIDAI KSA cover each other's token-level semantic content symmetrically.

nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: neutral-dominant (100.0%). "Ability to apply tradecraft to minimize risk of detection, mitigate risk, and mi" and "Skill in designing risk appetite statements for data and AI activities that are" are related but neither implies nor contradicts the other. Standard result for cross-framework mapping where two independent frameworks describe overlapping competencies with independent vocabulary.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.403 < STS 0.555 (gap -0.153). Cross-attention finds deeper alignment than independent encoding. The relationship depends on cross-text reasoning — concept-level mapping between [ability, apply, behavioral] and [accommodate, activities, ai] that bi-encoders structurally cannot capture.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 8.1%. Shared tokens: [risk]. DCWF-only: [ability, apply, behavioral, creation, detection, minimize]. WIDAI-only: [accommodate, activities, ai, appetite, business, cases].
